basic_info.title: The Witcher
basic_info.review: The Witcher is a groundbreaking RPG that introduced complex moral choices and mature storytelling to the genre, though its combat system shows its age compared to later entries in the series.

alternative_names: The Witcher: Enhanced Edition Director's Cut
The Witcher: Rise of the White Wolf (PS3/Xbox 360)

play_today.details: Enhanced Edition Director's Cut is the definitive version with all DLC, improved stability, and modern compatibility. Best experience when combined with community mods.

recommended_patches.name: HD Reworked Project
recommended_patches.description: Comprehensive visual overhaul improving textures, models, and assets for modern displays

recommended_patches.name: 4GB Patch (NTcore)
recommended_patches.description: Allows game to use more than 2GB RAM, preventing crashes on modern systems

recommended_patches.name: Aspect Ratio Fix
recommended_patches.description: Enables ultrawide and multi-monitor support for cutscenes

play_today.details: Console port with simplified controls but complete content. Good for casual play but lacks modding flexibility.
Console port with complete content and controller support. Performance similar to PS3 version.

description.synopsis: Geralt of Rivia, a mutated monster hunter known as a Witcher, investigates political conspiracies while searching for his former lover and navigating the dangerous world of magic and monsters.
key_features: Multiple difficulty levels affecting gameplay depth
Choice system with lasting consequences on story outcomes
First game in the acclaimed Witcher trilogy
description.long_description: The Witcher, released in October 2007 by CD Projekt Red (then known as CD Projekt), is based on Andrzej Sapkowski's bestselling fantasy novels. It was the studio's debut title and established their reputation for mature storytelling and complex moral choices. The Enhanced Edition Director's Cut (September 2008) added over 200 new animations, improved NPC models, redesigned inventory systems, reduced load times by 80%, and included all DLC content. While the original combat system shows its age compared to later entries in the series, the game remains praised for its narrative depth, world-building, and pioneering approach to player choice. The PS3 and Xbox 360 ports (2009) brought the experience to consoles but with simplified controls.
legacy_and_impact: Established CD Projekt Red as a major RPG developer
Introduced mature storytelling and moral complexity to Western RPGs
Created the foundation for one of gaming's most beloved franchises
Pioneered choice systems that influenced later RPGs like The Witcher 2 & 3
Continues to receive community support and mods years after release
